revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24756154998|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Izumi-jinja (Hitachi)]][[ja:泉神社 (日立市)]] {{Expand Japanese|泉神社 (日立市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Izumi Shrine (Q11554897) | native_name = 泉神社 | image = Izumi jinja izumi.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Izumi Shrine (Q11554897) | coordinates = {{coord|36.518549|140.624868}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Izumi Shrine'''|泉神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Hitachi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135022904|ltq=Q135022904|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11481683|ltq=Q11481683|qid=Q11481683|1=List of Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 9 ** P155: {{ill|Ameno Shirahano Shrine|lt=Ameno Shirahano Shrine|ja|天志良波神社|qid=Q11442597|1=Ame-no-Shirahano Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q11663648|ltq=Q11663648|qid=Q11663648|1=Shizu Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q633922|ltq=Q633922|qid=Q633922|1=Hitachi, Ibaraki}} * {{ill|Hitachi Province|lt=Hitachi Province|cs|Provincie Hitači|de|Provinz Hitachi|en|Hitachi Province|es|Provincia de Hitachi|fa|ولایت هیتاچی|fr|Province de Hitachi|gl|Provincia de Hitachi|hak|Hitachi-koet|id|Provinsi Hitachi|it|Hitachi (provincia)|ja|常陸国|ko|히타치국|lt|Hitačio žemė|nl|Hitachi (provincie)|pt|Província de Hitachi|ru|Хитати (провинция)|simple|Hitachi Province|sk|Hitači (provincia)|sr|Провинција Хитачи|tr|Hitachi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Хітаті|zh_min_nan|Hitati-kok|zh_yue|常陸國|zh|常陸國|qid=Q1055353|1=Hitachi Province}} * {{ill|Q1075212|ltq=Q1075212|qid=Q1075212|1=Kuji District, Ibaraki}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.518549|140.624868}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Izumi jinja izumi.JPG == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181762 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == P14005 (P14005) == * {{ill|Q11545372|ltq=Q11545372|qid=Q11545372|1=Senior Sixth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database * {{ill|Q11071125|ltq=Q11071125|qid=Q11071125|1=Junior Fifth Rank}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * アメノハヤタマヒメノ-Engishiki Jinmyōchō == Instagram username (P2003) == * izumi.jinja == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5050005007122 == Commons category (P373) == * Izumi-jinja (Hitachi) == said to be the same as (P460) == * {{ill|Q135039761|ltq=Q135039761|qid=Q135039761|1=Amenohayatamahimeno- Shrine}} ** P2868: {{ill|Shikinai Ronsha|lt=Shikinai Ronsha|ja|論社|qid=Q135022904|1=Shikinai Ronsha}} == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '茨城県日立市水木町2-22-1', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Izumi-jinja (Hitachi)}} [[JA:泉神社 (日立市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11554897}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Izumi Shrine |画像 = [[File:Izumi Jinja Hitachi Haiden.jpg|270px]]
Worship hall
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=13|frame-align=center|frame-width=270}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Hitachi|Q633922|qid=Q633922|1=Hitachi, Ibaraki}} Mizuki-chō 2-22-1 |祭神 = Amanohayatamahime-no-Mikoto |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (minor) candidate shrine
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown; one theory says year 49 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|qid=Q2547|1=May 3}} |神事 = Tōya Festival - old calendar Feb. 12–14
Hitachi no Sasara (Ibaraki Prefecture-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 31|緯度秒 = 6.79 |経度度 = 140|経度分 = 37|経度秒 = 29.67 |地図 = Japan Ibaraki |ラベル位置=left }} [[File:Izumi Jinja Hitachi Torii.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Torii}}]] [[File:Sandou of Izumi-jinja (Hitachi).jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Approach}}]] '''Izumi Shrine''' (いずみじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Hitachi|Q633922|qid=Q633922|1=Hitachi, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|Q83273|qid=Q83273|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. It is identified with “Amanohayatamahime-no-Mikoto Shrine” in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}},' and the spring in its precincts is described in the '{{ill|lt=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|Q11481687|qid=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}}' as “Ōi of Mitsuki no Sato.” Izumigamori, the shrine grove of evergreen chinquapin and pines, is designated a Historic Site of Ibaraki Prefecture. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following one. * Amanohayatamahime-no-Mikoto (あまのはやたまひめのみこと) According to 'Hitachi Nijūhassha-kō,' the Izumi River and sacred jewel are treated as the {{ill|lt=shintai|Q327532|qid=Q327532|1=Shintai}}. Amanohayatamahime-no-Mikoto is a deity not appearing in the {{ill|lt=Kiki|Q842308|qid=Q842308|1=Kiki (Shinto)}}. According to 'Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,' “Amanohayatamahime-no-Mikoto is a daughter of Amanotanabatahime-no-Mikoto, consort of {{ill|lt=Futodama|Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|qid=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}}, and is also called {{ill|lt=Amehiritome-no-Mikoto|Q85879124|qid=Q85879124|1=Ame no Hiratome}}”. “Hayatama” in the deity name is an honorific for clear spring water, and the principal deity is thought to be a deification of the spring called Ōi. In the 'Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku,' there is mention of “Amanohayatama-no-Kami” without the character for “hime,” but works such as 'Shikinaisha Chōsa Hōkoku' treat it as the same deity as Amanohayatamahime-no-Mikoto. === History === The founding date is unknown. There is a theory that it was founded in year 49 of {{ill|lt=Emperor Sujin|Q333530|qid=Q333530|1=Emperor Sujin}} by Funase no Sukune, the {{ill|lt=Kuji no Kuni no Miyatsuko|Q11369664|qid=Q11369664|1=Kuji Kokuzō}}, but Funase no Sukune belonged to the time of {{ill|lt=Emperor Seimu|Q316672|qid=Q316672|1=Emperor Seimu}}, and this has been pointed out as a later fabrication. Izumigamori in the precincts is identified with “Ōi of Mitsuki no Sato” in the Kuji District section of the '{{ill|lt=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|Q11481687|qid=Q11481687|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki}},' and Izumi Shrine is identified with “Amanohayatamahime-no-Mikoto Shrine” listed in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'. Note that the 'Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki' contains no direct description of Izumi Shrine. According to the '{{ill|lt=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Q1052981|qid=Q1052981|1=Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku}},' Amanohayatama-no-Kami was promoted from Senior Sixth Rank Upper to Junior Fifth Rank Lower on May 27, {{ill|lt=866|Q23872|qid=Q23872|1=866}} (Jōgan 8), and from Junior Fifth Rank Lower to Junior Fifth Rank Upper on December 29, {{ill|lt=874|Q23843|qid=Q23843|1=874}} (Jōgan 16). In the '{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=UNKNOWN}},' it is listed in Kuji District, Hitachi Province, as “Amanohayatamahime-no-Mikoto Shrine,” ranked as a minor shrine. After the Meiji Restoration, in April Meiji 6 (1873), it was ranked as a Gōsha under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine rank system|Q712534|qid=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. According to an extant {{ill|lt=munafuda|Q8008834|qid=Q8008834|1=Munafuda}}, in {{ill|lt=1530|Q6429|qid=Q6429|1=1530}} (Kyōroku 3), {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshiatsu|Q11383465|qid=Q11383465|lt=Satake Yoshiatsu|1=Satake Yoshiatsu}} repaired the shrine buildings and changed the shrine title to Izumi Daimyōjin. In Eiroku 3, {{ill|lt=Satake Yoshiaki|Q11383447|qid=Q11383447|1=Satake Yoshiaki}} reroofed the buildings, and in the Edo period, under Mito Domain protection, construction was carried out on June 26, Kan'en 3. Later, the shrine buildings burned down in the Kyōwa era (1801–1804) and were rebuilt in the Bunka era (1804–1818). In 1960 (Shōwa 35), they burned down again, and a temporary shrine building was constructed in 1961 (Shōwa 36). According to 'Hitachi Taga-gun-shi,' before the loss, the main sanctuary was 9 shaku wide and 8 shaku deep, and the worship hall was 4 ken 3 shaku in front and 2 ken 3 shaku in depth. === Precincts === [[File:Izumi Jinja Hitachi Honden.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Main sanctuary}}]] The precinct area is 1,185 tsubo (3,917 m²), and the outside-owned land is 6,655 tsubo (22,000 m²). The shrine buildings constructed in 1961 (Shōwa 36) are: a reinforced-concrete nagare-zukuri main sanctuary, 1 ken 2 shaku wide and 2 ken deep; and a worship hall with corrugated-metal-roof irimoya-zukuri, 3 ken 3 shaku wide and 3 ken deep. The entire Izumi Shrine precinct is called Izumigamori and is designated a historic site of Ibaraki Prefecture. It consists of evergreen trees such as chinquapin and pine. The “Ōi of Mitsuki no Sato” in the Kuji District section of the 'Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki' is identified with Izumigamori. In the northeast part of Izumigamori is a spring with a circumference of about 40 meters and maximum depth of about 2 meters. This spring deepens sharply toward the center like a mortar and has more than 20 spring vents of various sizes. === Precinct shrines === Precinct shrines are as follows{{Cite web|ja|date= |url=http://izumi-jinja.sakura.ne.jp/Yuisyo-Page.html |title=History of Izumi Shrine |publisher=Izumi Shrine |accessdate=2015-7-13}}. * Itsukushima Shrine - Ichikishimahime-no-Mikoto * Mitsumine Shrine - Izanagi-no-Kami, Izanami-no-Kami * Sagimori Shrine - Amatamahashiraya-hime-no-Mikoto * Fuji Shrine - Konohanasakuya-hime-no-Mikoto * Hōnen Shrine - Tsukuyomi-no-Mikoto, Ōanamuchi-no-Mikoto * Inari Shrine - Uka-no-Mitama-no-Ōkami File:Mitsumine-jinja in Izumi-jinja (Hitachi).jpg|Mitsumine Shrine File:Itsukushima-jinja in Izumi-jinja (Hitachi).jpg|Itsukushima Shrine File:Goushaden of Izumi-jinja (Hitachi).jpg|Sagimori Shrine / Fuji Shrine / Hōnen Shrine / Inari Shrine === Festivals === [[File:Mikoshiden of Izumi-jinja (Hitachi).jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Mikoshi hall}}]] * New Year Festival - January 1 * Tsuina Festival - February 3 * Spring Festival (Kinen-sai) - February 11 * Tōya Festival - old calendar Feb. 12–14 * Annual Festival - May 3 * Autumn Festival (Niiname-sai) - November 23 === Cultural properties === === Prefecture-designated Intangible Folk Cultural Property === * Hitachi no Sasara - designated August 23, 1963 (Shōwa 38). === Prefecture-designated Historic Site === * {{ill|lt=Izumigamori|Q11554674|qid=Q11554674|1=Izumigamori}} - designated December 1, 1969 (Shōwa 44). === Access === ; Address : Mizuki-chō 2-22-1, Hitachi, Ibaraki Prefecture ; Transportation : Located about 1 km northeast of {{ill|lt=Ōmika Station|Q39951|qid=Q39951|1=Ōmika Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Jōban Line|Q1369263|qid=Q1369263|1=Jōban Line}}. === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist|2|refs= 'Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer,' pp. 188–191. 'Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 11 Tōkaidō 6,' pp. 490–496. 'Ibaraki Prefecture Encyclopedia,' pp. 45–46. 'Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 8: Place Names of Ibaraki Prefecture,' pp. 100–101. 'Kadokawa Japanese Place Name Dictionary 8: Ibaraki Prefecture,' pp. 111–112. 'Notes on Shrines of Ibaraki I,' pp. 137–139. 'Notes on Shrines of Ibaraki II,' pp. 228–230. }} === Bibliography === *{{Cite book|ja |author = Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer Compilation Committee (ed.) |year = 1973 |title = Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Gazetteer |publisher = Ibaraki Prefecture Shrine Office |isbn = }} *{{Cite book|ja |author = Shikinaisha Study Group (ed.) |year = 1976 |title = Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol. 11 Tōkaidō 6 |publisher = Kōgakkan University Press |isbn = }} *{{Cite book|ja |author = |year = 1981 |title = Ibaraki Prefecture Encyclopedia |publisher = Ibaraki Shimbun |isbn = }} *{{Cite book|ja |author = |year = 1982 |title = Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei 8: Place Names of Ibaraki Prefecture |publisher = Heibonsha |isbn = }} *{{Cite book|ja |author = |year = 1983 |title = Kadokawa Japanese Place Name Dictionary 8: Ibaraki Prefecture |publisher = Kadokawa Shoten |isbn = }} *{{Cite book|ja |author = Keiji Kamata |year = 1986 |title = Notes on Shrines of Ibaraki I |publisher = |isbn = }} *{{Cite book|ja |author = Keiji Kamata |year = 1993 |title = Notes on Shrines of Ibaraki II |publisher = |isbn = }} === External links === * [https://izumi-jinjya.com/ Izumi Shrine] - Official website ** {{Twitter|izm12680463}} ** {{Instagram|izumi.jinja}} {{神道 横}}{{茨城県の神社一覧目次}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Izumi Shrine (Hitachi)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Izumi Shrine (Hitachi)]][[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:日立市の歴史]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Hitachi, Ibaraki]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]]
